findings.title: JWT revocation mechanism not implemented
findings.description: Gallery admin JWTs lack a revocation system. Currently reduced to 7-day expiry (from 30 days) as a workaround, but compromised tokens cannot be revoked before expiry. This creates a security window where leaked credentials remain valid.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement JWT blacklist using a DynamoDB table with TTL. On logout or admin removal, add token to blacklist. On verify_jwt, check blacklist before accepting token. Use token expiry as TTL value for auto-cleanup.
findings.evidence: # TODO: Gallery admin JWTs have no revocation mechanism. Reduced from 30 to 7 days
findings.impact: Security risk: leaked admin credentials remain active until expiry. Reduces trust in multi-user team setups where sub-admins need to be removed quickly.

findings.title: Client email allowlist validation incomplete
findings.description: Client access control has a TODO for per-gallery email allowlist validation. Currently clients can access galleries if they verify email, but there's no per-gallery allowlist to restrict access to specific email addresses.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add optional 'allowed_client_emails' array to gallery record. In auth_view handler, after email verification, check if email is in allowlist (if defined). Return 403 if email not in allowlist. Add UI in admin to manage per-gallery allowlist.
findings.evidence: # TODO (CLIENT-03): Validate email against a per-gallery allowlist or
findings.impact: Security/privacy: Photographers cannot restrict client gallery access by email. Any verified client can potentially access galleries they shouldn't.

findings.title: Missing API error documentation and error response standardization
findings.description: API responses lack consistent error format and there is no API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger spec). Errors are inconsistent: some return {'error': 'message'}, others {'message': '...'}, and error codes vary.
findings.fix_suggestion: Define canonical error response schema: {status, error_code, message, details, request_id}. Audit all Lambda handlers and normalize responses. Generate OpenAPI 3.0 spec from annotations. Publish to /api/docs endpoint.
findings.evidence: Multiple error response formats: respond(404, {'error': '...'}), respond(503, {'message': '...'}), respond(400, {'errors': {...}})
findings.impact: Integration friction: third-party developers and frontend maintainers must reverse-engineer API behavior. Clients can't reliably parse errors. Reduces platform extensibility.

findings.title: No client password reset or account recovery flow
findings.description: Clients access galleries via email verification tokens (JWT), but if they lose access or want to change settings, there's no account recovery flow. No self-service password reset, no account settings management for clients.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add client account settings page (optional, post-auth). Allow clients to set email preferences, download history. Implement 'forgot email' flow that sends verification link to registered email. Add account deletion (GDPR).
findings.evidence: Gallery.html provides read-only client interface. No account management, settings, or recovery options.
findings.impact: UX friction: clients locked out have no self-service recovery path. Support burden increases. Clients feel powerless.

findings.title: Admin Lambda handler is oversized and difficult to maintain
findings.description: Single handler.py file in admin Lambda is 5,951 lines. Combines gallery management, upload orchestration, ChromaHaus orchestration, comments, bans, jobs, and client management. No modular structure.
findings.fix_suggestion: Refactor into logical modules: gallery_crud.py, photo_management.py, chromahaus.py, comments.py, jobs.py, auth.py. Use a router pattern to dispatch to modules. Reduce main handler to <500 lines.
findings.evidence: 5951 lines in single file. Functions range from 10 to 500+ lines. No clear separation of concerns.
findings.impact: Maintenance burden: difficult to reason about, high cognitive load for new developers, increased bug risk when making changes. Harder to test individual features in isolation.

findings.title: No gallery-level analytics or insights
findings.description: Admin can see access logs and download counts, but no insights: top photos, engagement trends, client visit frequency, peak times. Data is collected but not exposed as actionable insights.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add analytics endpoint that aggregates access logs: views_by_date, downloads_by_photo, most_viewed_photos, client_engagement_score. Add sparklines to admin dashboard. Build a 'Gallery Insights' panel.
findings.evidence: get_gallery_stats() returns basic photo/video counts but no time-series data, engagement metrics, or trend analysis.
findings.impact: Growth friction: photographers can't optimize based on client behavior. Missed opportunity to show value.

findings.title: Error messages are generic and unhelpful
findings.description: API returns generic errors like 'Gallery not found' or 'Something went wrong' without context. Frontend often shows these raw to users. No request IDs for support troubleshooting.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add request_id to all responses. Enhance errors with context: 'Gallery XYZ not found. It may have expired or been deleted.' Add error codes (GALLERY_NOT_FOUND, CHROMAHAUS_TIMEOUT). Return actionable recovery steps.
findings.evidence: respond(404, {'error': 'Gallery not found'}) — no context on why, what to check, or request ID for debugging
findings.impact: UX friction: users frustrated by unhelpful errors. Support can't easily debug issues. Error doesn't guide users on next steps.

findings.title: Missing end-to-end test coverage
findings.description: Test suite has 271 unit tests (good coverage), but no E2E tests. No integration tests for full flows: create gallery, upload, process, review, publish, client view.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add E2E test framework (Playwright). Create tests for: admin wizard flow, client auth & download, comment moderation. Run E2E tests in CD pipeline before deploy.
findings.evidence: tests/ has only unit/ and smoke/ directories. Smoke tests are minimal. No E2E test suite.
findings.impact: Risk: regressions in multi-step flows aren't caught until production. Wizard flow deployed without E2E tests.

findings.title: No structured request logging or tracing
findings.description: Lambda functions use basic logging, but no request IDs, trace headers, or structured logging (JSON). CloudWatch logs are difficult to correlate across functions.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement X-Request-ID header through all Lambda handlers. Use structured logging (JSON) with context (user_id, gallery_id, request_id). Integrate with CloudWatch Insights.
findings.evidence: logger = logging.getLogger() — basic Python logging, no request context, no trace IDs
findings.impact: Debugging friction: hard to trace user requests through system. Slow incident response. Poor observability.

findings.title: Missing loading states in UI
findings.description: Admin interface has some loading indicators, but not consistently. Uploading files, processing with ChromaHaus, generating zips show some progress but lack estimated time.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add loading skeleton for all async operations: zip generation, ChromaHaus processing. Show estimated time if available. Add 'Cancel' button for long operations.
findings.evidence: Upload handlers show progress, but zip generation and some async operations lack clear loading state.
findings.impact: UX friction: users unsure if operation is stuck. May accidentally retry or navigate away.

findings.title: No batch operations or bulk edit features
findings.description: Photographers can hide individual photos, but no batch operations: bulk hide, bulk style override, bulk move to trash.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add batch selection (Ctrl+Click, Shift+Click) and bulk action menu: hide, unhide, trash, apply style. Show count selected.
findings.evidence: Photo curation allows per-photo operations but no bulk edit. Gallery details form is single-gallery edit only.
findings.impact: UX friction: tedious for large galleries. Photographers with 100+ photos must click each individually.

findings.title: No scheduled alerts for gallery expiry
findings.description: Galleries auto-expire after TTL, but photographers aren't notified. No reminders or 'X days until expiry' warnings.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add scheduled Lambda job to check galleries expiring in 7, 3, 1 days. Send SES email notifications. Add 'extend expiry' button in admin.
findings.evidence: Maintenance Lambda exists but has no expiry alert job.
findings.impact: UX friction: photographers surprised when galleries disappear. Support burden increases.

findings.title: Dependency updates available
findings.description: Current dependencies have patch and minor version updates: Pillow, numpy, cryptography. No update cadence documented.
findings.fix_suggestion: Pin all dependencies to specific versions. Add quarterly dependency update review. Automate with Dependabot.
findings.evidence: Pillow>=10.0.0 (latest: 11.x), cryptography==42.0.8 (latest: 44.x)
findings.impact: Technical risk: missed security patches, compatibility issues with newer libraries.

findings.title: No API documentation or integration guide
findings.description: No OpenAPI spec, no API docs site. Developers must read code to understand endpoints.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create OpenAPI 3.0 spec. Build docs site with Swagger UI. Add integration guide, authentication flow, error codes, rate limits.
findings.evidence: docs/ has domain migration and handoff notes but no API reference.
findings.impact: Platform friction: third-party integrations impossible without reverse-engineering.

findings.title: No confirmation dialogs for destructive actions
findings.description: Delete gallery, permanently delete comments, trash operations lack confirmation dialogs. Users can accidentally delete galleries.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add modal confirmation for all destructive actions: 'Delete gallery? This cannot be undone.' Require typing gallery name for high-impact operations.
findings.evidence: Delete functions may not all have confirm() checks before destructive operations.
findings.impact: UX risk: accidental data loss. Photographers delete galleries by mistake with no recovery.

findings.title: No undo mechanism for published galleries
findings.description: Once photos are approved and published, there's no undo or recall. Photographers must delete and recreate.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add 'grace period': approved photos are live after 5-min delay. During grace period, photographers can revoke. After grace period, add 'unpublish' button.
findings.evidence: approve_staging() marks photos as 'approved' with no reverse operation.
findings.impact: UX friction: rigid workflow. Photographers want to fix typos or retract approved photos.

findings.title: Limited guidance for first-time photographers
findings.description: Wizard is new and good, but onboarding after first gallery is minimal. No tips or guided tours.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add onboarding checklist: 'Invite clients', 'Share link', 'Customize comments'. Add tooltips for key features.
findings.evidence: Wizard exists, but main dashboard doesn't guide photographers on next steps.
findings.impact: Activation friction: photographers unsure what to do after first gallery.

findings.title: No structured error logging in Lambda handlers
findings.description: Lambda handlers log exceptions but not in structured format. No error tracking service integration.
findings.fix_suggestion: Integrate error tracking (Sentry). Log all errors with context: user_id, gallery_id, operation.
findings.evidence: except Exception: pass — errors silently swallowed or logged inconsistently
findings.impact: Debugging friction: errors go unnoticed. No error trends.

findings.title: No webhook system for third-party integrations
findings.description: No webhooks to notify external systems when galleries are created or published. Limits third-party potential.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add webhook subscription table. When gallery events occur, dispatch to registered URLs. Add webhook management UI.
findings.evidence: No webhook infrastructure in handlers or Terraform.
findings.impact: Platform friction: third-party apps must poll. Reduces extensibility.

findings.title: Opportunity: Gallery analytics and insights dashboard
findings.description: Photographers collect valuable data but don't see insights: top photos, client engagement, download trends.
findings.fix_suggestion: Build analytics dashboard: views_by_photo, downloads_by_photo, client_visits_by_date. Add gamification: badges, trends.
findings.evidence: Access logs exist but aren't aggregated into insights.
findings.impact: Growth: photographers see value. Upsell opportunity.

findings.title: Opportunity: Social sharing and embeddable galleries
findings.description: Currently galleries require login. Could support public preview mode or embeddable galleries on photographer websites.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add 'public mode': no login required, view-only, optional passcode. Generate embeddable code.
findings.evidence: Gallery requires email verification. No public/preview mode.
findings.impact: Growth: photographers can showcase work without login friction.

findings.title: Opportunity: Mobile app or PWA for photographers
findings.description: Photographers spend time on-the-go. No mobile app or PWA to manage galleries.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add PWA manifest and service worker. Build Expo app (React Native) for iOS/Android with push notifications.
findings.evidence: Admin interface is responsive but not optimized for mobile. No PWA support.
findings.impact: Growth: photographers check galleries while shooting.

findings.title: Theme consistency between marketing site and admin panel
findings.description: Marketing site uses warm gold, admin panel uses purple. Login page still uses gold. Inconsistent branding.
findings.fix_suggestion: Unify theme: update login page to match admin panel's purple OR update admin to match marketing gold.
findings.evidence: Login page theme: gold. Admin panel theme: purple.
findings.impact: UX friction: confusing visual transition. Feels like different products.

findings.title: Database schema lacks indexes for common queries
findings.description: DynamoDB tables use basic key schemas. No GSI for querying by created_at or status.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add GSI on created_at for galleries. Add GSI on access_key for access logs.
findings.evidence: gallery_access_log has no date-based GSI. Photos stored as nested array.
findings.impact: Performance: full table scans for common queries. Cost increases.

findings.title: No advanced search or filtering on admin side
findings.description: Photographers can search galleries by name, but can't filter by date, status, client, or style.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add filter sidebar: date range, status, client email, default style. Add 'Save search' for smart folders.
findings.evidence: Gallery search only searches by name. No filter UI for status, date, client email.
findings.impact: UX friction: photographers with many galleries hard to find specific ones.

findings.title: CloudFront cache invalidation strategy not documented
findings.description: Cache invalidation logic scattered across Lambdas. No documented strategy or audit trail.
findings.fix_suggestion: Centralize cache invalidation logic. Document patterns for each operation. Add audit logging.
findings.evidence: Multiple Lambdas call CloudFront invalidate, but no centralized strategy.
findings.impact: Maintenance friction: hard to reason about cache behavior. Risk of stale content.

findings.title: No API rate limiting enforcement or documentation
findings.description: API endpoints lack rate limiting. No throttling strategy documented.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement rate limiting: 100 req/min per API key. Use DynamoDB. Document limits in API docs.
findings.evidence: No rate limiting middleware or Lambda concurrency reservations documented.
findings.impact: Risk: abuse potential. No protection against scrapers.

findings.title: Zen product positioning on marketing site lacks implementation roadmap
findings.description: Analogify Zen is positioned as a separate consumer product, but it's marketing-only. No shipped app or implementation timeline.
findings.fix_suggestion: Deprioritize Zen marketing until shipped, OR define MVP scope and timeline. If shipping, add waitlist.
findings.evidence: Zen landing page exists, but no backend or shipping timeline documented.
findings.impact: UX friction: customers can't download Zen. Could damage trust.

findings.title: ESLint Configuration Missing — Blocks CI/CD
findings.description: ESLint v9 requires an eslint.config.js file. Currently, 'npm run lint' fails immediately with 'ESLint couldn't find an eslint.config.(js|mjs|cjs) file.' This blocks the CI/CD pipeline because buildspec-ci.yml runs 'npm run lint' and will fail on all PRs.
findings.evidence: Running 'npm run lint' returns: 'ESLint: 9.39.4 — ESLint couldn't find an eslint.config.(js|mjs|cjs) file.' The buildspec-ci.yml calls 'npm run lint' in the build phase, which will fail for all PRs.
findings.impact: Blocks all pull requests from passing CI. No code can be merged until linting is fixed. This is a hard blocker for contributions.

findings.title: Vitest Discovers Unrelated Tests in Terraform Module
findings.description: Vitest is configured to run all tests and finds TypeScript test files in terraform/.terraform/modules/codebuild/tests/ that have no tsconfig.json. These cause test runs to fail with 'TSCONFIG_ERROR'. The tests aren't part of auth-service and shouldn't be included.
findings.evidence: Running 'npm test' shows: 'FAIL terraform/.terraform/modules/codebuild/tests/products.test.ts [TSCONFIG_ERROR] Failed to load tsconfig'. These tests are in a vendored Terraform module and not part of auth-service's codebase.
findings.impact: Test output is cluttered with unrelated failures. Coverage reports are incomplete. Developers may get confused about what's actually being tested.

findings.title: No Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
findings.description: The service provides no MFA/2FA capabilities. Users with compromised passwords have no secondary factor to prevent account takeover. This is a standard security feature for authentication services.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password) support: 1) Add 'totp_secret' and 'totp_enabled' to user profile. 2) Create /api/auth/mfa/setup endpoint to generate TOTP secret (QR code). 3) Create /api/auth/mfa/verify endpoint to confirm TOTP during login. 4) Store backup codes for account recovery. 5) Add mfaEnabled flag to token payload. Alternatively, start with SMS using a service like Twilio.
findings.evidence: Login handler (line 393-456) accepts only email and password. No support for TOTP, SMS, or backup codes anywhere in the codebase.
findings.impact: High-value accounts are vulnerable to password compromise. Users cannot add extra security. Reduces product competitiveness vs. other auth services that offer MFA by default.

findings.title: No Session Management or Token Revocation
findings.description: Users cannot log out, revoke tokens, or view active sessions. JWTs are valid for 30 days with no way to invalidate them before expiration. This means users cannot force logout a compromised session.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement session management: 1) Create 'SESSIONS' table in DynamoDB with session ID, user ID, created_at, expires_at, last_activity, user_agent, ip_address. 2) Create /api/auth/logout endpoint to mark session as revoked. 3) Create /api/auth/sessions endpoint (GET) to list active sessions, (DELETE) to revoke by session ID. 4) Store session ID in JWT instead of just user ID. 5) On each request, verify session exists and hasn't been revoked. 6) Optional: Implement activity tracking and suspicious login detection.
findings.evidence: JWT created with 30-day expiration (line 142: 'exp: now + 86400 * 30'). No logout endpoint exists. No token blacklist or session table to track active sessions.
findings.impact: Users cannot log out. If a token is leaked/stolen, it remains valid for 30 days. No audit trail of who is signed in. Users cannot kick off suspicious sessions.

findings.title: No Social Login (OAuth Providers)
findings.description: The service only supports email/password login. Modern users expect social login via Google, GitHub, Apple, etc. This increases friction and abandonment during signup.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add OAuth 2.0 provider support: 1) Choose providers (Google, GitHub, Apple). 2) Register app with each provider, get client IDs/secrets stored in Secrets Manager. 3) Create /api/auth/oauth/authorize endpoint (redirects to provider). 4) Create /api/auth/oauth/callback endpoint (handles provider token exchange). 5) Link OAuth identity to user account (or auto-create if new). 6) Return JWT like normal login. Consider using a library like Passport.js or Auth.js to simplify.
findings.evidence: Only /api/auth/register and /api/auth/login endpoints exist. No OAuth flow, no provider configuration, no identity federation.
findings.impact: Higher signup friction. Users who prefer social login are less likely to complete registration. Reduces user base relative to competitors offering Google/GitHub login.

findings.title: No Audit Trail or Activity Logging
findings.description: User actions (login, password change, email change, account deletion) are logged to CloudWatch as structured JSON, but there's no queryable audit table. Admins have no way to review user activity, see failed login attempts, or investigate suspicious behavior.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create an AUDIT_LOG table in DynamoDB with PK='AUDIT#YYYY-MM-DD', SK='HH:MM:SS#UUID', storing: event_type, user_id, email, action, ip_address, user_agent, status (success/failure), timestamp, details. Create /api/admin/audit endpoint (GET with date range filter) to retrieve logs. Optionally encrypt logs or store in S3 for long-term retention.
findings.evidence: Events are logged as console.log with JSON (e.g., line 388: 'auth_register', line 441: 'auth_login_success'). But no database table stores these events for querying. No admin endpoint to retrieve audit history.
findings.impact: Cannot perform security investigations. Cannot detect compromised accounts. Admin has no visibility into user behavior. Violates compliance best practices (GDPR article 32 requires audit logs).

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on Admin Endpoints
findings.description: Admin endpoints (/api/admin/*) lack rate limiting. An attacker with admin access (or who compromises an admin token) can perform unlimited operations like listing all users, resetting passwords, or disabling accounts.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add rate limiting to admin endpoints: 1) Rate limit per admin user (not per IP, since admins may use VPNs). 2) List users: 10 requests/minute. 3) Reset password: 5 per user per hour. 4) Toggle status: 5 per user per hour. 5) Return 429 with Retry-After header. Use same checkRateLimit function as auth endpoints but with admin-specific limits.
findings.evidence: handleAdminRoute() receives user, path, method but no rate limiting checks. User endpoints like /api/admin/users/list-users scans entire table without limits. resetPassword and toggleStatus have no rate limits.
findings.impact: Admin panel is a privileged operation — if compromised, attacker has unlimited access. Cannot prevent abuse by malicious admins or compromised tokens. No protection against brute-force enumeration of users.

findings.title: Silent Email Failures Without User Notification
findings.description: If the Resend API key is not set or the Resend API returns an error, the function logs a warning but continues and returns 200 OK. Users are not informed that their verification/reset email failed to send.
findings.fix_suggestion: 1) Change sendEmail to throw an error on failure instead of returning false. 2) Wrap email sends in try-catch at handlers. 3) If email fails, return 503 Service Unavailable or 500, not 200. 4) Inform user: 'Email service unavailable. Please try again later.' 5) For registration, create the user first, then send email. If email fails, return 500 but user is created (so they can try /api/auth/resend-verification later). Or implement retry logic with exponential backoff.
findings.evidence: sendEmail() function (lines 42-74) checks 'if (!apiKey) { console.warn(...); return false; }'. The result is logged but callers ignore the boolean return value. For example, in handleRegister (line 375), the email send result is not checked.
findings.impact: Users complete registration but never receive verification email. Password reset codes are sent to CloudWatch but not to users. Accounts become unverifiable. Users are confused why their email doesn't arrive.

findings.title: No Comprehensive Error Code Taxonomy
findings.description: API returns generic error messages like 'Invalid email or password' and 'Invalid reset code'. Clients cannot distinguish between different failure modes (expired, already used, rate limited, invalid format, etc.).
findings.fix_suggestion: Add standardized error codes to responses: { statusCode: 401, error: { code: 'AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS', message: 'Invalid email or password' } }. Define a taxonomy: AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS, AUTH_EMAIL_UNVERIFIED, AUTH_RATE_LIMITED, AUTH_ACCOUNT_DISABLED, VALIDATION_INVALID_EMAIL, VALIDATION_PASSWORD_TOO_SHORT, RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT_EMAIL_EXISTS, etc. Update all handlers to return proper codes.
findings.evidence: Error responses use plain text: { error: 'Invalid email or password' } (line 418, 431). No error codes like 'AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS', 'AUTH_EMAIL_UNVERIFIED', 'AUTH_RATE_LIMITED', etc.
findings.impact: Client apps cannot provide granular error handling. Cannot distinguish 'try again later' from 'email not found'. Cannot localize error messages properly. Hard to debug integrations.

findings.title: No Request ID Tracking in API Responses
findings.description: API responses don't include a request ID. If an error occurs, users have no unique identifier to share with support for debugging. CloudWatch logs have requestId from API Gateway, but it's not returned to the client.
findings.fix_suggestion: Pass requestId through to all responses: 1) Extract requestId from event.requestContext.requestId. 2) Add it to the res() function. 3) Include in every response as { requestId: '...', data: {...} }. 4) Helps support correlate logs.
findings.evidence: handler() receives event with requestContext.requestId but never includes it in response (line 124-130 res() function).
findings.impact: Support debugging is harder. User says 'I got an error' but there's no correlation ID to look up logs. Reduces product professionalism.

findings.title: No Disabled User Error Handling in Password Reset
findings.description: When a user resets their password via /api/auth/reset-password, the code checks if the user exists but does not verify if the user account is disabled. A disabled user can reset their password and re-enable themselves.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a disabled check before password reset: After querying user (line 580), check 'if (user.disabled) return res(403, { error: 'Account is disabled' }, origin);'. This prevents disabled users from resetting and regaining access.
findings.evidence: handleResetPassword() (line 530-591) queries user by email, finds the account, and resets password without checking user.disabled flag. Compare to login handler (line 424) which properly checks 'if (user.disabled)'.
findings.impact: Disabled accounts can be reactivated by anyone who has the email. If an admin disabled an account due to abuse, the user can just reset password and regain access.

findings.title: No API Documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)
findings.description: No OpenAPI/Swagger specification exists. Developers integrating with the auth-service must manually read the Lambda code or reverse-engineer the API.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create an openapi.yaml file documenting all endpoints: /api/auth/register, /api/auth/login, /api/auth/forgot-password, /api/auth/reset-password, /api/auth/verify-email, /api/auth/password, etc. Include request/response schemas, error codes, rate limits. Host OpenAPI spec at /openapi.json endpoint. Consider setting up Swagger UI or ReDoc at /docs.
findings.evidence: No openapi.json, swagger.json, or Swagger/Redoc endpoints. No @openapi annotations in code.
findings.impact: High friction for API consumers. No interactive API explorer. Cannot auto-generate SDKs. Integration documentation is out-of-date.

findings.title: Email Change Doesn't Preserve Encryption Keys
findings.description: When a user changes their email via PUT /api/auth/email, the password is re-hashed with a new salt and encryption keys are kept. However, the email change flow requires re-verification, which causes the emailVerified flag to be set to false. If the user had encryption setup, this creates an inconsistent state.
findings.fix_suggestion: Decide on email change behavior: Option 1 (Current): Re-verify email, preserve encryption. This is OK if client handles it. Option 2: Preserve verification status (don't re-verify). Option 3: Delete encryption keys on email change (safest, most secure). Document the choice clearly. If keeping current behavior, ensure client apps handle unverified email with active encryption.
findings.evidence: handleChangeEmail() (line 742-808) sets 'emailVerified: false' (line 776) when changing email. But encryption keys (encryptedMasterKey, etc.) are preserved. This means a user with verified email and encryption suddenly has unverified email but encryption still active.
findings.impact: Potentially confusing state for end users. Encryption recovery depends on email verification status. May cause unexpected behavior in client apps.

findings.title: Legacy Password Salt Handling Adds Complexity
findings.description: The code maintains support for legacy passwords hashed with a hardcoded salt ('tnbm_salt_2024'). On login, legacy passwords are detected and automatically migrated to per-user random salts. This is good practice but adds maintenance burden.
findings.fix_suggestion: This is actually a good practice. However, consider: 1) Set a migration deadline (e.g., 6 months). After that, reject any login attempt that triggers legacy salt (force password reset). 2) Add a migration status report to admin panel to see how many users have been migrated. 3) Once migration is complete, remove legacy salt handling entirely.
findings.evidence: LEGACY_PASSWORD_SALT constant (line 8). verifyPassword() checks 'if (!user.salt)' and uses legacy salt (line 192). migrateLegacyPassword() is called on login (line 435) if detected.
findings.impact: Extra code paths and complexity. If legacy salt is ever exposed, all old passwords are compromised. Ongoing maintenance burden to keep migration code.

findings.title: Hardcoded App Registry Without Runtime Configuration
findings.description: The APP_REGISTRY (app names, domains, email addresses) is hardcoded in lambda/api/index.mjs. Adding a new app requires code change and redeployment.
findings.fix_suggestion: Move APP_REGISTRY to Secrets Manager or a DynamoDB config table. Load at startup (cached). Add a /api/admin/apps endpoint to list registered apps. Optionally allow dynamic app registration.
findings.evidence: APP_REGISTRY constant (lines 12-23) with thenewbeautifulme and selah hardcoded. Same in admin.mjs (lines 78-81). To add a new app, must edit code and redeploy.
findings.impact: Inflexible. Cannot add new tenants without deployment. Tightly couples infrastructure to product config.

findings.title: No TypeScript — Reduced Code Safety
findings.description: The codebase is JavaScript without types. This increases runtime errors and makes IDE support/autocomplete minimal. As the service grows, type safety becomes increasingly important.
findings.fix_suggestion: Migrate to TypeScript: 1) Add typescript to devDependencies. 2) Rename lambda/api/index.mjs → index.ts. 3) Add type annotations for function parameters and returns. 4) Create types/ directory for shared types (User, AuthRequest, etc.). 5) Set up tsconfig.json. 6) Gradually migrate tests. This is a larger effort but improves code quality significantly.
findings.evidence: All files are .mjs (ECMAScript modules) with no .ts files. No type annotations or JSDoc types. package.json has no typescript dependency.
findings.impact: Higher defect rate. IDE support is limited. Refactoring is risky. New developers have to infer types from code. Type errors only caught at runtime.

findings.title: No Setup/Deployment Guide for New Developers
findings.description: README.md documents the stack and CI/CD at a high level, but doesn't provide step-by-step instructions for: setting up local development environment, running tests, configuring Secrets Manager, or understanding the database schema.
findings.fix_suggestion: Expand README.md with sections: 'Getting Started' (clone, npm install, set env vars), 'Development' (npm run lint, npm test, local testing), 'Database' (DynamoDB schema explanation, key patterns), 'Secrets' (how to set JWT_SECRET, RESEND_API_KEY locally), 'Deployment' (how CD works, terraform apply manually if needed).
findings.evidence: README.md (36 lines) provides overview and CI/CD info but no 'Getting Started', 'Development Setup', 'Database Schema', or 'Deployment' sections.
findings.impact: New developers onboarding is slow. Harder to contribute. Mistakes when setting up local environment.

findings.title: Rate Limit Errors Don't Include Retry-After in Response Body
findings.description: When a user hits rate limits, the API returns a 429 status with Retry-After header, but the response body doesn't include the retry-after value. Users can read the header, but JSON clients need to parse headers explicitly.
findings.fix_suggestion: Include retryAfter in response body: { statusCode: 429, body: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Too many attempts', retryAfter: 123 }) } where 123 is seconds from now.
findings.evidence: handleRegister() rate limit response (lines 318-322) sets Retry-After header but body is just { error: '...' } with no retryAfter field.
findings.impact: Client developers have to read headers (awkward in some frameworks). Some clients may not retry correctly. UX is worse when user sees 'too many attempts' with no retry timing info.

findings.title: Forgot Password Anti-Enumeration Silently Absorbs Invalid Emails
findings.description: The /api/auth/forgot-password endpoint returns 200 OK for both valid and invalid emails (anti-enumeration). This is correct from a security standpoint but creates poor UX if a user typos their email — they won't get the reset email but won't know why.
findings.fix_suggestion: This is a trade-off between security and UX. Best practice: Return 200 for all cases (current), but in the frontend, ask user to confirm their email. If they don't receive email in 5 minutes, provide a 'try again with different email' option. Alternatively, ask for email and password to confirm the account exists (reduces enumeration risk).
findings.evidence: handleForgotPassword() (line 459) always returns safeResponse (200, 'If an account exists...') for all cases. Users cannot tell if their email was found (lines 461-462, 497).
findings.impact: User experience is confusing. User assumes they got the email. Waiting for reset email that never comes creates support burden.

findings.title: No Validation on Admin Allowlist Email Format
findings.description: The admin allowlist from Secrets Manager is not validated. If a malformed email is added to the allowlist, the isAdmin() function will still accept it, but it won't match real user emails.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add validation in getAdminAllowlist(): after parsing JSON, validate each email format matches /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/. Log warnings for invalid emails. Consider rejecting startup if allowlist is invalid.
findings.evidence: getAdminAllowlist() (lines 14-33) parses JSON from Secrets Manager but doesn't validate email format. Line 37: 'return allowlist.includes(email.toLowerCase().trim());' will do string matching on whatever is in the list.
findings.impact: Admin access could be blocked if allowlist is misconfigured. No validation at deploy time. Hard to debug.

findings.title: No Password Strength Policy Beyond Length
findings.description: The service enforces minimum password length (10 chars) but no complexity rules (uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols). Weak passwords like 'aaaaaaaaaa' are accepted.
findings.evidence: handleRegister() checks 'if (password.length < 10)' (line 307) but no complexity validation. Similarly in handleResetPassword() (line 533).
findings.impact: Users can set weak passwords that are easy to brute-force. Slightly increases account compromise risk.

findings.title: No Account Recovery Without Email Access
findings.description: If a user loses access to their registered email, there's no way to recover the account. They cannot request a recovery code, answer security questions, or verify phone number.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add recovery options: 1) Backup phone number during signup (optional). 2) Pre-set security questions. 3) Recovery codes generated during account setup (download/print). 4) Social account recovery (if OAuth added). 5) Support-verified recovery (provide ID, answer questions). Start with recovery codes (simplest, most secure).
findings.evidence: All recovery paths use email: forgot-password (line 272), verify-email (line 285), change-email (line 288). No phone-based or question-based recovery.
findings.impact: Account is permanently locked if email is lost/inaccessible. Support team has no self-service recovery path. Increases support burden.

findings.title: No API Key Management for Service-to-Service Authentication
findings.description: Only JWT tokens are supported for auth. There's no way for external services or third-party apps to authenticate via API keys. All auth flows require user interaction.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add API key support: 1) Create APIKEY table in DynamoDB. 2) Generate API keys for users (/api/auth/api-keys POST). 3) Add /api/auth/api-keys GET/DELETE endpoints. 4) Modify getUser() to accept both JWT and API key in Authorization header. 5) Rate limit API key usage separately.
findings.evidence: getUser() (line 162) only checks Authorization: Bearer token header. No API key header support.
findings.impact: Cannot build integrations or API consumers. Third-party apps cannot use auth-service. Reduces platform potential.

findings.title: Webhook Support for Event Notifications
findings.description: The service has no webhook or event system. Downstream apps cannot be notified when users sign up, verify email, reset password, or delete accounts.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add webhook support: 1) Create WEBHOOKS table to store registered endpoints. 2) Add /api/admin/webhooks endpoint to register/manage hooks. 3) Publish events to SNS when user actions occur (registered, verified, deleted). 4) Lambda subscriptions to SNS invoke registered webhook URLs. 5) Implement retry logic with exponential backoff. 6) Add webhook secret signing for security.
findings.evidence: Events are logged to CloudWatch (console.log) but not published to SNS, SQS, or webhooks. No subscription system for event consumers.
findings.impact: Integrations require polling or direct database access. Hard to keep systems in sync. Reduced extensibility.

findings.title: Identity Provider (OpenID Connect / OAuth2 Provider)
findings.description: Currently auth-service is an authentication service for BreakPoint Labs apps. It could be extended to become an identity provider so other services delegate authentication to it (like Auth0).
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement OIDC provider: 1) Add /oauth/authorize endpoint (redirects to login form). 2) Add /oauth/token endpoint (exchanges auth code for JWT). 3) Add /oauth/userinfo endpoint (returns user profile). 4) Add /.well-known/openid-configuration for discovery. 5) Register client apps with client_id/client_secret. 6) This is a significant undertaking; consider using ory/hydra or keycloak as reference implementation.
findings.evidence: No OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow, no OpenID Connect endpoints, no discovery document (.well-known/openid-configuration).
findings.impact: All BreakPoint Labs apps currently handle auth independently. auth-service could be shared identity provider, reducing duplication.

findings.title: Role-Based Access Control Beyond Admin Allowlist
findings.description: Currently, admin access is binary (in allowlist or not). There's no fine-grained permissions (read-only, write, delete, etc.). A proper RBAC system would enable more flexible admin delegation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement RBAC: 1) Create ROLES table with role_id, permissions array. 2) Add role to user profile. 3) Create permission checks: canListUsers, canResetPassword, canDeleteUser, etc. 4) Modify admin endpoints to check specific permissions. 5) Add /api/admin/roles endpoint to manage roles.
findings.evidence: isAdmin() (line 35) returns true/false. No concept of roles (view-only-admin, support-admin, super-admin, etc.). All admins have full access.
findings.impact: Cannot delegate limited admin tasks. All admins have maximum privileges. Increases insider risk.

findings.title: Analytics and Insights Dashboard
findings.description: The service generates lots of data (user registrations, logins, password changes, email verifications) but has no analytics dashboard. Product teams cannot see metrics like daily signups, email verification rate, or password reset frequency.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add metrics and dashboard: 1) Publish CloudWatch Metrics for key events (RegisterCount, LoginSuccessCount, LoginFailureCount, PasswordResetCount, etc.). 2) Create CloudWatch dashboard showing daily/hourly trends. 3) Optionally add /api/admin/analytics endpoint returning aggregated stats. 4) Set up CloudWatch alarms for anomalies (spike in failed logins, low verification rate, etc.).
findings.evidence: Events logged to CloudWatch JSON but no aggregation, no dashboard, no metrics published to CloudWatch Metrics.
findings.impact: Product visibility is limited. Cannot measure conversion funnels, identify drops in onboarding, or react to spikes in account issues.

findings.title: No Internationalization (i18n) Support
findings.description: All email templates and error messages are hardcoded in English. Users in other locales receive English emails even if their app supports their language.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add i18n support: 1) Detect user language from X-Accept-Language header or user profile. 2) Create i18n JSON files for each language (en.json, es.json, fr.json, etc.). 3) Load appropriate language. 4) Replace hardcoded strings with i18n keys. 5) Allow users to set language preference in profile.
findings.evidence: Email templates hardcoded (line 378, 516, etc.). Error messages in English. No i18n library or language negotiation.
findings.impact: Poor UX for international users. Emails are in English even if app is translated.

findings.title: Health Check Endpoint Is Minimal
findings.description: The /api/health endpoint (line 267) returns { status: 'ok' } without checking dependencies (DynamoDB, Secrets Manager). If DynamoDB is down, the health check still returns 200.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enhance health check: 1) Perform a DynamoDB ListTables or GetItem on a test record. 2) Check Secrets Manager availability. 3) Return { status: 'ok|degraded', checks: { dynamodb: 'ok', secretsManager: 'ok' } }. 4) Return 503 if critical dependencies fail.
findings.evidence: GET /api/health returns { status: 'ok' } without any readiness checks.
findings.impact: Load balancers and monitoring tools think the service is healthy even if it cannot serve traffic. Can mask outages.

findings.title: Missing SECURITY.md for Security Policy and Reporting
findings.description: No SECURITY.md file documenting security practices, vulnerability reporting process, or responsible disclosure.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create SECURITY.md with: 1) Security practices (password hashing, CORS, rate limiting, etc.). 2) Vulnerability reporting email (security@codyjo.com or similar). 3) Responsible disclosure timeline. 4) Supported versions for security patches.
findings.evidence: No SECURITY.md in root. No reference to vulnerability reporting process. GitHub doesn't show security policy.
findings.impact: Security researchers don't know how to report vulnerabilities privately. Public issues may expose sensitive information.

findings.title: Foundation for Advanced Features Is In Place
findings.description: The service has a solid foundation for expansion: JWT support, encryption key management, DynamoDB schema with GSI, rate limiting, admin panel. These are good building blocks for MFA, sessions, RBAC, webhooks, etc.
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue building on this foundation. Prioritize MFA, session management, and audit logs next. Document architectural decisions in ARCHITECTURE.md so future builders understand the design.
findings.evidence: Code is clean, well-structured, and includes rate limiting, CORS, Secrets Manager integration, and encryption key storage. Architecture supports multi-tenant apps.
findings.impact: Technical debt is low. Future features can be added without major refactoring. Good velocity on new features.

findings.title: Multi-Tenancy Is Well-Designed
findings.description: The service supports multiple apps (thenewbeautifulme, selah) through X-App-Origin header and APP_REGISTRY. The data model is tenant-agnostic (users are identified by UUID, not scoped to app). This is good design for a shared service.
findings.fix_suggestion: Maintain this architecture. Document in ARCHITECTURE.md how app registration works. Consider dynamic app registration (from config table) instead of hardcoded registry.
findings.evidence: APP_REGISTRY (line 12) supports multiple apps. Email templates customize by app. Users are globally unique by ID. Data model doesn't tie users to app.
findings.impact: Can easily add new BreakPoint Labs apps without duplication. Good scalability.

findings.fix_suggestion: Add retry with exponential backoff, localStorage cache, and user-friendly errors.
findings.evidence: No retry logic or error handling
findings.impact: API outages cause app to appear broken.

findings.title: Reading plans local-only — no sync
findings.description: Plans stored in localStorage, no account sync or reminders.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add /api/reading-plans CRUD and sync on login/logout.
findings.evidence: Plans in localStorage key, no API endpoint
findings.impact: Users lose progress on device changes; reduces habit formation.

findings.title: Missing loading skeletons
findings.description: Data-heavy pages show no skeleton loading states.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create Skeleton component and use on History, Insights, Study pages.
findings.evidence: Content pops in without placeholder
findings.impact: Layout shift harms accessibility and SEO.

findings.title: No validation library
findings.description: Forms lack type safety; API responses not validated.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Zod for runtime validation of forms and API responses.
findings.evidence: No Zod, forms cast without validation
findings.impact: API changes can silently break frontend.

findings.title: Internationalization not implemented
findings.description: App is English-only with hardcoded strings.
findings.fix_suggestion: Install next-intl and extract strings to translation files.
findings.evidence: All strings in English, no i18n library
findings.impact: Cannot serve Spanish, French, Portuguese speakers.

findings.title: Social login not implemented
findings.description: Only email/password auth; no Google, Apple, Facebook login.
findings.fix_suggestion: Integrate Cognito or auth0 for OAuth flows.
findings.evidence: No OAuth endpoints or provider integration
findings.impact: Higher sign-up friction vs competitors.

findings.title: Plausible only tracks pageviews
findings.description: No custom events for key actions.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add custom events for study, bookmark, journal, share actions.
findings.evidence: No trackEvent() calls for feature usage
findings.impact: Cannot measure adoption or conversion funnel.

findings.title: Email digests not implemented
findings.description: No way to send reminder or weekly digest emails.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add email reminder UI, API, and EventBridge scheduler.
findings.evidence: No reminder scheduling API
findings.impact: Inactive users have no re-engagement mechanism.

findings.title: No destructive action confirmation
findings.description: Delete buttons lack confirmation dialogs.
findings.fix_suggestion: Show confirmation modal and extend undo to 10s.
findings.evidence: Delete calls API directly, 5.5s undo window
findings.impact: Users lose data to accidental clicks.

findings.title: Study page navigation unclear
findings.description: Study page shows 'coming soon' but links to books.
findings.fix_suggestion: Clarify landing page and complete guided study feature.
findings.evidence: Confusing UI with placeholder text
findings.impact: Users confused about how to start learning.

findings.title: DynamoDB schema not documented
findings.description: Single-table design with composite keys but no schema docs.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create docs/DATA-MODEL.md with schema and query examples.
findings.evidence: Composite keys used without documentation
findings.impact: Onboarding developers slow; risk of incorrect queries.

findings.title: Daily verse buttons not mobile-optimized
findings.description: Copy/share buttons too small for mobile; no gestures.
findings.fix_suggestion: Increase tap targets to 48px, add swipe gestures, keyboard shortcuts.
findings.evidence: 16px icons, no gesture handlers
findings.impact: Mobile users struggle; low accessibility.

findings.title: No error.tsx fallback pages
findings.description: App lacks error boundaries for runtime errors.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create error.tsx with user-friendly UI and Sentry logging.
findings.evidence: No error.tsx files
findings.impact: White screen on error with no recovery.

findings.title: No Storybook documentation
findings.description: Components lack visual documentation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Install Storybook and document key components.
findings.evidence: No .stories.tsx files
findings.impact: Slower development and component reuse.

findings.title: No notification center
findings.description: Cannot notify users in-app of updates.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add notification center with message queue and API.
findings.evidence: No notification component
findings.impact: Users miss feature announcements.

findings.title: No community features
findings.description: App is personal-only; no social or leaderboard features.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add optional friend feature, community studies, leaderboards.
findings.evidence: No friend or community views
findings.impact: Limits social engagement for community Christians.

findings.title: Voice features not wired
findings.description: VoiceReader component exists but not integrated.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add speaker button and text-to-speech controls.
findings.evidence: Component defined but not used
findings.impact: Vision-impaired and commute users cannot engage.

findings.title: No prayer tracking system
findings.description: Journal has prayers but no 'answered' status tracking.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add prayer_status field and 'mark answered' UI.
findings.evidence: No prayer_status field
findings.impact: Users cannot track prayer answers.

findings.title: No accountability partner features
findings.description: App lacks partnership features for community Christians.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add partner invites and shared progress tracking.
findings.evidence: No partner connection model
findings.impact: Cannot invite accountability partners.

findings.title: CSV export mentioned but not implemented
findings.description: CLAUDE.md mentions CSV export but no UI exists.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add CSV export endpoint and UI button.
findings.evidence: JSON export only, no CSV option
findings.impact: Data portability incomplete.

findings.title: Blog content limited
findings.description: Blog page has 3 hardcoded posts, no CMS.
findings.fix_suggestion: Integrate Notion or Contentful for blog content.
findings.evidence: Hardcoded posts, no CMS integration
findings.impact: Blogging channel static and hard to grow.

findings.title: Wallpaper feature underutilized
findings.description: Verse wallpaper only on Daily Verse, not on all verses.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add wallpaper button to Books, Study, Bookmarks pages.
findings.evidence: Logic exists but only called from Daily page
findings.impact: Users don't discover feature.

findings.title: No A/B testing infrastructure
findings.description: Cannot run experiments to measure feature impact.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add feature flags and experiment tracking.
findings.evidence: No feature flag system
findings.impact: Slower product iteration.

findings.title: No podcast integration
findings.description: No sermon or podcast content integration.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add podcast and audio Bible integration.
findings.evidence: No podcast player
findings.impact: Users must leave app for related content.

findings.title: No curriculum/course structure
findings.description: Plans exist but no structured curriculum with milestones.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create course model with lessons and certificates.
findings.evidence: Plans are linear lists, no course model
findings.impact: Users seeking structured learning must go elsewhere.

findings.title: Test coverage critically low at 30%
findings.description: Only 30% of application code is covered by tests. This means 70% of the codebase could have bugs, breaking changes, or regressions that go undetected.
findings.fix_suggestion: Phase 1: Add 20 tests for critical paths (auth, readings, journal). Phase 2: Reach 60% coverage with component tests. Phase 3: Add integration/E2E tests for 80%.
findings.evidence: vitest coverage: components 11.61%, lib 40.26%, app pages 0%. Total ~30%.
findings.impact: Deployment risk is high. Untested pages like Reading, Journal, History, Insights lack regression protection.

findings.title: Push/email notifications not implemented despite settings UI
findings.description: Settings shows toggles for daily reminders, weekly reviews, milestone celebrations. Users enable them but nothing happens.
findings.fix_suggestion: Option A: Remove UI until implemented. Option B: Implement via EventBridge + Resend email.
findings.evidence: Preferences stored in localStorage but no Lambda sends notifications. CLAUDE.md lists as backlog.
findings.impact: Users enable reminders expecting delivery but nothing happens. Erodes trust.

findings.title: i18n framework built but no translations deployed
findings.description: i18n supports en, es, fr, pt but no translation files exist. Framework silently falls back to English.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create translation files for es, fr, pt. Integrate useI18n() into components.
findings.evidence: Framework exists but src/locales/ directory is empty.
findings.impact: Non-English speakers cannot use app. International growth blocked.

findings.title: PWA/offline mode not functional
findings.description: capacitor.config.ts suggests mobile/offline plans but service worker doesn't exist. App requires constant internet.
findings.fix_suggestion: Phase 1: Add service worker with cache-first for static, network-first for API. Phase 2: Add offline UI. Phase 3: Queue mutations offline, sync online.
findings.evidence: Config exists but no src/service-worker.ts. References to navigator.serviceWorker exist but SW missing.
findings.impact: App is internet-dependent. Users cannot access previously loaded content offline.

findings.title: Blog, card detail, and guide pages missing loading skeletons
findings.description: Card/guide/blog pages lack skeleton loaders while content streams in. Creates perceived slowness.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add PageSkeleton component. Show while content loads, switch to real content.
findings.evidence: No Skeleton components on content pages, unlike insights/page.tsx.
findings.impact: Perceived slowness on content pages. Blog/SEO content key to growth but has poor UX.

findings.title: Error messages not user-facing (console.error without toast)
findings.description: Errors logged to console but not shown to users. Silent failures with zero feedback.
findings.fix_suggestion: Show toast: 'Failed to load insights. Please refresh.' or gracefully degrade.
findings.evidence: 20+ console.error calls without showToast.
findings.impact: Users see broken features with no explanation. May leave app.
